context: Tests whether the agent implements a financial audit logger service with correct tamper-detection hash chaining, a single write entry point, and proper before/after state capture. The agent is asked to write a TypeScript audit logger module for an ecommerce backend.

checklist.name: SHA-256 hash computation
checklist.description: The audit logger computes a SHA-256 hash for each event using Node's built-in 'crypto' module (createHash('sha256'))

checklist.name: Hash input fields
checklist.description: The hash is computed from exactly these fields joined by '|': prev seq (or 0), occurred_at, event_type, aggregate_id, actor_id, JSON.stringify(after_state)

checklist.name: prev_hash chaining
checklist.description: Each event stores the hash of the previous event for the same aggregate (prev_hash), retrieved by querying the last event ordered by seq DESC before inserting

checklist.name: Single write method
checklist.description: All audit writes go through a single method (e.g. record() or similar) — no direct db.insert calls on the audit table outside this method

checklist.name: before_state captured pre-mutation
checklist.description: The before_state snapshot is fetched BEFORE the mutation executes (not after), so it reflects the record's state prior to the change

checklist.name: both before_state and after_state stored
checklist.description: Both before_state and after_state are stored on mutation events — not just the delta field alone

checklist.name: actor_ip captured
checklist.description: The actor's IP address is recorded on audit events (actor_ip field), not just the user/actor ID

checklist.name: correlation_id included
checklist.description: A correlation_id (derived from the HTTP request context) is stored on audit events to link events from the same request

checklist.name: delta computed separately
checklist.description: The delta field contains only the changed fields (with from/to values), computed by comparing before_state and after_state keys

checklist.name: seq used in hash
checklist.description: The previous event's seq number (not id/UUID) is used as part of the hash input to maintain ordering and gap-detection semantics

checklist.name: typed event helpers
checklist.description: Typed domain-specific helper functions are provided for at least two common financial event types (e.g. orderCreated, paymentCaptured, refundIssued, manualPriceAdjustment)
